events.title: Screen Time with Toronto Public Health
events.description: In this session, the benefits and impacts of screen time will be explored. Discussion will include looking at recommendations for screen time per age of the child (between 0 and 6 years of age) and how to model and manage appropriate screen time behaviour. Physical activity recommendations as an alternative to screen time will be reviewed.

events.title: From the Golden Age to the Modern Era: A Musical Odyssey of East Asia
events.description: This concert introduces the history of the yangqin and its diverse regional and folk traditions, guiding the audience on a musical journey across China through sound, storytelling, and performance. This performance will include selections from different eras and styles, along with brief introductions to provide an overview of the musical journey. Les familles sont invitées à rester et à jouer pour 30 minutes supplémentaires après la conclusion du programme. Enjoy stories, songs and rhymes in French at a staff-led story time for children age 0-5 with their parents and caregivers. Families are welcome to "stay and play" for an additional 30 minutes following the program. -------

events.title: Toddler Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs and rhymes in this program for children, 19 months to 3 years, and their parents or caregivers. Toddler Time runs from 10 a.m. to 10:30 a.m.

events.title: EarlyON Family Resource Program
events.description: Learn about quality community resources for young families, enjoy a circle time for the young ones, and get some nutritious snacks. Suitable for families with children birth to six years old.  In partnership with EarlyON Child and Family Centre of Regent Park Community Health Centre.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for a half hour of stories, songs, and rhymes. For children ages 5 and under along with their parents and/or caregivers. Family Time runs from 10:30-11am. Children, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play until 11:30am.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers.

events.title: Together Time
events.description: This is a program for parents and caregivers with children from birth - 6 years. Families have the opportunity to socialize while children develop new skills in an early learning environment.

events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! Drop-in.  March Break Challenge: Build a mall as a group from 2-3pm If you identify as a person with a disability and require an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services by email, accessibleservices@tpl.ca, or voicemail, 416-393-7099, to make a request. Please contact us at le

events.title: Baby and Toddler Story Time
events.description: Join us for bouncing rhymes, songs and stories in this program for babies, birth to 3 years, and their parents or caregivers. Baby and Toddler Story Time runs from 10:00 to 10:30. Children, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play, from 10:30 to 11:00.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for rhymes, songs and stories for children from 0-5, with their parents or caregivers. A fun activity, game or craft to follow. Children, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs and rhymes in this program for children ages 5 years and under, and their parents or caregivers. Family Time runs from 10:30 to 11:00 am. Stay and Play from 11:00 to 11:30 am Drop-in. No registration required.

events.title: The Dr. Bonkers Show
events.description: Join Dr. Bonkers as the colourful entertainer brings joy to kids and families! With music and instruments, Dr. Bonkers and his best puppet friend, Larry, teach lessons about friendship, kindness, and imagination. Through vibrant sing-alongs and fun antics, Dr. Bonkers offers an unforgettable experience filled with laughter, music, and dancing! Ages 0-5. Drop-in.

events.title: Baby Time
events.description: Join us for bouncing rhymes, songs and stories in this program for babies, birth to 18 months, and their parents or caregivers. Drop in, no registration required.

events.title: Baby Time
events.description: Join us for bouncing rhymes, songs and stories in this program for babies, birth to 18 months, and their parents or caregivers. Baby Time runs from 1:00 PM to 1:30 PM. Children, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play, from 1:30 PM to 2:00 PM.

events.title: Storytelling and Afrikan Drumming
events.description: Storyteller and professinal percussionist, Kwanza Msingwana, performs folktales and traditional stories from Afrika and around the world. This is a drop-in program. No registration is required.

events.title: Game on! Board Game Playtime
events.description: Join us for a fun-filled day of board games and puzzles! Play your favourites or try something new! Brings your friends and family or meet new people at the library. Games available for use in the Children's Area. Available games include: Ball Fall Jumbo (Ages 3+), STEM Pretend Flower Garden Toy Set (Ages 3-6), Operation (Ages 6+), STEM Road Builder Game (Ages 4-8), Dinosaur Tetra Tower Balance Stacking Blocks Game (Ages 6+) Available puzzles include: Giant World Map Puzzle (

events.title: Crafturday
events.description: Join us for a day of fun and crafts. Partake in a new and engaging craft every Saturday from 9:30am - 4:30pm. Drop-in and self-serve. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. If your child lives with a disability, and requires an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services at accessibleservices@tpl.ca or phone 416-393-7099. Please contact us at least three weeks in advance.

events.title: Blocks Challenges
events.description: Join us on a Saturday morning for a building activity! Complete the challenges using Mega Bloks and Lego Duplo blocks. events.title: Scientists in School: Science Meets Art
events.description: Discover what happens when science meets art! Explore perception as you use shadows, colour, motion, and light to create art from science. You’ll make a puppet, a spinning top and a kaleidoscope, as well as design your own galaxy. “The greatest scientists are always artists as well!” (Albert Einstein) Please note: this program is suitable for children 4 years old and above with adult accompaniment. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. If your child lives with a disability, and requires an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services at accessibleservices@tpl.ca or phone 416-393-7099. Please contact us at least three weeks in advance.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers.  Drop-in, registration is not required. This is a year-round program.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers. Family Time runs from 10:30 to 11:30. Drop in. No registration required, but space is limited.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for a fun storytime with stories, songs and rhymes followed by a craft or activity. For children ages 5 and under with their parents or caregivers.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers. Family Time runs from 11:00AM  to 11:30AM .  Accessibility Live or automated closed captioning, sign language interpretation, intervenor services, or attendant services are available upon request. Please submit your request at least 2 weeks in advance to: tpl.ca/accessibility-events .

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers. Family Time runs from 11:00am to 11:30am. Children, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play, from 11:30am to 12:00pm.

events.title: Builder's Club
events.description: Join us on Saturday mornings and build to your heart’s content!  LEGO, DUPLO, and KEVA planks will be provided for you to explore your creativity. All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Blocks Challenges
events.description: Join us on a Saturday morning for a building activity! Complete the challenges using Mega Bloks and Lego Duplo blocks. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. Drop-in and self-serve. Unfortunately, we cannot accommodate any large groups/daycares for this program. If you or your child lives with a disability, and requires an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services by email accessibleservices@tpl.ca , or voicemail, 416

events.title: Sunday Crafternoon
events.description: Join us for Sunday crafts. Different activity each week. Sundays 12-4:30 pm. Drop-in.

events.title: Baby Time
events.description: Bouncing and tickling rhymes, songs and stories for babies from birth to 18 months with their parents or caregivers. Drop-in; no registration required. Space is limited.

events.title: Toddler Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs and rhymes in this program for children, 19 months to 3 years, and their parents or caregivers.

events.title: EarlyON Family Resource Program
events.description: Learn about quality community resources for young families, enjoy a circle time for the young ones, and get some nutritious snacks. Suitable for families with children birth to six years old.  In partnership with EarlyON Child and Family Centre of Regent Park Community Health Centre.

events.title: Baby Time
events.description: Bouncing and tickling rhymes, songs and stories for babies from birth to 18 months with their parents or caregivers. Baby Time runs from 10:30 to 11:00am. Babies, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play until 11:30am.

events.title: Together Time
events.description: This is a program for parents and caregivers with children from birth - 6 years. Families have the opportunity to socialize while children develop new skills in an early learning environment.

events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! Drop-in.  March Break Challenge: Build a mall as a group from 2-3pm If you identify as a person with a disability and require an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services by email, accessibleservices@tpl.ca, or voicemail, 416-393-7099, to make a request. Please contact us at le

events.title: Baby Time
events.description: Bouncing and tickling rhymes, stories, and songs for babies from birth to 18 months with their parents or caregivers.

events.title: Baby and Toddler Story Time
events.description: Join us for bouncing rhymes, songs and stories in this program for babies, birth to 3 years, and their parents or caregivers. Baby and Toddler Story Time runs from 10:00 to 10:30. Children, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play, from 10:30 to 11:00.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for rhymes, songs and stories for children from 0-5, with their parents or caregivers. A fun activity, game or craft to follow. Children, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs and rhymes in this program for children ages 5 years and under, and their parents or caregivers. Family Time runs from 10:30 to 11:00 am. Stay and Play from 11:00 to 11:30 am Drop-in. No registration required.

events.title: Game on! Board Game Playtime
events.description: Join us for a fun-filled day of board games and puzzles! Play your favourites or try something new! Brings your friends and family or meet new people at the library. Games available for use in the Children's Area. Available games include: Ball Fall Jumbo (Ages 3+), STEM Pretend Flower Garden Toy Set (Ages 3-6), Operation (Ages 6+), STEM Road Builder Game (Ages 4-8), Dinosaur Tetra Tower Balance Stacking Blocks Game (Ages 6+) Available puzzles include: Giant World Map Puzzle (

events.title: Unconventional Materials: An Osborne Collection Exhibit
events.description: An exhibition of intricately crafted books, artworks and much more. The Unconventional Materials exhibit highlights the use of unconventional techniques and materials in children’s literature, featuring historical and modern examples.   Visit the Osborne Collection to experience our exhibit featuring items such as plasticine art by Barbara Reid, dioramas and panoramas, postcards and other ephemera, and hornbooks and battledores. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Henna Art with Hina
events.description: Celebrate Asian Heritage Month and discover the art of henna, decorative designs which are created on a person's body, using a paste of natural organic henna. Observe and enjoy the henna application and learn about its history, recipe and traditions. Led by Henna Artist, Hina Yasir. No registration required, all ages, first come, first serve! Come by early to secure your spot, taking place on the main floor of the library.

events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. Diagram of Saturn's Moons by Frank R. Paul, 1953.

events.description: Do you like to build with LEGO, DUPLO, Feltro, Snap Circuits or KEVA? Join us every Saturday morning for some fun building challenges! All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Swansea Centennial Open House
events.description: Drop into the library for our Open House to celebrate 100 years of Swansea Village. Pick up a time capsule craft, view our photo display, and participate in a scavenger hunt with prizes. While supplies last.

events.title: Rainbow Day
events.description: Celebrate Pride Month at Toronto Public Library! Come celebrate Pride Month at our Rainbow Day – a fun-filled program that celebrates the 2SLGBTQ+ community with crafts, games and music! For families with kids 12 and under. 10:30: Rainbow Storytime 11:00-1:00: Rainbow Crafts 12:00 to 3:00: Pride Themed Scavenger Hunt

events.title: Builder's Club

events.title: Blocks Challenges
events.description: Join us on a Saturday morning for a building activity! Complete the challenges using Mega Bloks and Lego Duplo blocks. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. Drop-in and self-serve. Unfortunately, we cannot accommodate any large groups/daycares for this program. If you or your child lives with a disability, and requires an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services by email accessibleservices@tpl.ca , or voicemail, 416

events.title: Author Talk: Where the Lightning has Struck, A true story about Mohawk Chief John Deserontyon
events.description: Join Pamela Devonshire as she shares her children's book, "Where the Lightning Has Struck: A True Story About Mohawk Chief John Deserontyon," including why she wrote her book and why it's important to honour and remember her ancestry. Children will then be invited to create their own bookmarks where they can write thank you in Mohawk or the language of their choice and share about their own culture and heritage, too. Space is limited. Registration is required on tpl.ca starting May 30th.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: STEAM Saturday: Rigamajig
events.description: Join us for a hands-on building adventure with Rigamajig — a giant creative construction set made of wooden planks, wheels, pulleys, and bolts! events.title: Builder's Club

events.title: BenTastic Fantastic Magic Show
events.description: Find your "inner magic" during this upbeat, hilarious, and interactive magical experience with BenTastic! Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as he leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "real magicians" during this fast-paced, fun and amazing magic show! Tickets will be available at 1:30 pm. There is a limit of 60 participants and priority given to families with children.

events.title: Club de lecture de bande dessinée
events.description: *English text follows* Pratiquez votre français pendant les vancances d'été ! Chaque mois, lisez une nouvelle bande dessinée en français de votre choix en lien avec le thème proposé, et venez nous en parler. Bien sûr, nous nous amuserons aussi avec diverses activités. Le thème du mois de juillet est la magie . Choisissez un livre parmi ceux mentionnés ci-dessous ou tout autre ouvrage correspondant au thème . Les sorcières de Brooklyn de Sophie Escabasse L'île des mythes de Anaïs Halard

events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno.  Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Chess and Board Game Café Drop In
events.description: Join us for a relaxed session of games and good company. We’ve got chess sets and a great selection of board games ready to play. Whether you're into strategy or just here for some laughs, all skill and interest levels are welcome!

events.title: Sunday STEAM Stations
events.description: Build, create, learn & play at various stations including Snap Circuits, LEGO, KEVA Planks, Connectors, & Colouring! This is a program for kids, teens, & families.  All are welcome!  Drop-in, no registration required. Please note: there is no program on Sun April 5, as TPL will be closed for the Easter Sunday holiday.

events.title: Sunday Family Movies
events.description: For the Month of April we'll be hosting Sunday Family Movies! Bring friends, family and a snack to wind-down while we watch movies anyone can enjoy.  Drop-in  All ages. The program will take place in the Auditorium located in the basement. Elevator on site. If you have any questions, please call Bendale Branch at 416-396-8910.

events.title: 3D Design with TinkerCAD
events.description: Learn the basics of 3D design with Tinkercad - a free web app for everyone. By combining simple shapes, it is possible to create complex designs and potentially, 3D print the result with ease. Participants will create a custom keychain to be 3D printed and available for pick-up in the days following the program. Prerequisites : Mouse and keyboarding skills required. events.title: Youth Hub: NBA2K Tournament
events.description: Join Weston Youth Hub for the NBA2K Tournament! Play against your peers, battle for first place, and have fun! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Game Day
events.description: Drop in on Mondays to challenge your friends to a match of Mario Kart or FIFA, or chill out with some solo play. events.title: History of Generative Music: The Last 50 Years

What began as experiments in university labs has become technology that anyone can access from a laptop or phone. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. Diagram of Saturn's Moons by Frank R. Paul, 1953.

Tables and chairs are provided, as well as a variety of materials to take study breaks. This is a drop-in program for Teens, and space is limited.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: Indigenous Film Screening - The Stand
events.description: On a misty morning in the fall of 1985, a small group of Haida people blockaded a muddy dirt road on Lyell Island, demanding the government work with Indigenous people to find a way to protect the land and the future. In a riveting new feature documentary drawn from more than a hundred hours of archival footage and audio, award-winning director Christopher Auchter ( Now Is the Time ) recreates the critical moment when the Haida Nation’s resolute act of vision and conscience changed the world. In

events.title: Toronto Games Week: An Intro to DaggerHeart
events.description: In celebration of Toronto Games Week (June 11 - Jun 17) come learn the ins and outs of the Tabletop Roleplaying phenomenon known as DaggerHeart. Players will fall into the roles of our heroic adventurers and prove they have the strength, skills, knowledge and bravery to become the Games Week Champions! No prior Tabletop Roleplaying experience required! Come with an open mind and an open heart! (Program intended for a mature audience.) Information Collection Notice: The personal information on th

events.title: Throwback Thursdays: Great Movies on the Big Screen
events.description: Come join us for a radical retro rewind projected on the big screen. Whether you're a first-time viewer or long-time fan, these movies are sure to please. April 16: Meet the Parents (2000, PG) April 23: Rounders (1998, R) April 30: No Country for Old Men (2007, 18A) May 7: Apollo 13 (1995, PG) May 14: The Martian (2015, PG) * special start time! 5:30 pm May 21: Contact (1997, PG) * special start time! 5:30 pm May 28: Star Trek (2009, PG) All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Silent Book Club
events.description: Come bring a book you are reading. We read for 30-45 minutes and then everyone talks about what they have read. We meet the second Thursday of every month from 7:00 to 8:00 pm.

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Thursday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. This year we are reading:  February 19 -  The Strangers by Katherena Vermette April 16 -  Big Little Lies by Liane Moriarty June 18 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 17 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 19 -  Small Things Like These by

events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around the Branch Tour
events.description: The Parkdale branch and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood tour. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. The tour will start and end at the Parkdale branch in the branch lobby area and will be guided by the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff. This program takes place rain or shine. events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: D&D for Teens: On-going Campaign
events.description: Play Dungeons and Dragons! Slay monsters and overcome challenges with a Librarian as your Dungeon Master. Ongoing Campaign. This is an in-person program for Teens. Space limited for players*, but watchers are welcome too. All supplies provided. Bring a 5th level character or pick a Pre-generated character. *Register in-person at the Youth Hub at 3:30PM. events.title: Friday Teen Social
events.description: Drop in after school on Friday's to have fun with your friends, meet new folks, build connections, and tell us about programs and services you would like to see in the neighborhood!

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Join us at the Bloor/Gladstone Library to play chess with friends, family, and fellow enthusiasts! Players of all ages and strengths are welcome, no previous experience is required. Drop in. No registration required.

events.title: Swansea Centennial Open House
events.description: Drop into the library for our Open House to celebrate 100 years of Swansea Village. Pick up a time capsule craft, view our photo display, and participate in a scavenger hunt with prizes. While supplies last.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Create Pop Music with AI Tools for Teens
events.description: Have you ever had a melody stuck in your head and wished you could turn it into a real song? AI tools are making it easier than ever to bring your musical experiences to life - but how do you make sure the music you create is truly yours? Explore how to use AI as a creative assistant to build radio-ready pop tracks based on your own original ideas. You'll learn how to guide AI tools to serve your vision rather than replace it, and we'll cover Canadian copyright guidelines to ensure the music you

events.title: How to Become a Volunteer
events.description: Interested in learning more about volunteering opportunities in Toronto? This session is intended for the volunteer seekers who are new to volunteering, or in need to understand the process of formal volunteering. Join us to learn how and where to search for opportunities that best match your interests and what to expect after you apply for a volunteer role. This session caters to all demographics. Presented by Volunteer Toronto .

events.title: Teen Study Hall

events.title: Music Copyright and Ethics in the AI Era
events.description: When you use AI to create music, who actually owns it? Can a melody generated by a machine infringe on someone else's copyright? As AI tools become more accessible, these questions are more relevant than ever - and the answers aren't always straightforward. In this session, you'll get a plain-language breakdown of how music copyright works, what the rules are around sampling, and how these long-standing principles apply to AI-generated music. events.title: Game On!
events.description: Level up your free time at the library after school! Teens are invited to drop in, hang out, and connect with friends in a relaxed, welcoming space. Dive into some casual play or friendly competition with popular video games for Switch and PS4/5, or enjoy some unplugged fun with board games when you need a screen break.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Games

If there is no demand, gaming will be awarded on a first come first serve basis.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer.

events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Youth Hub:  Nail Night

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Soundscape
events.description: Next we will be inspired by the library’s architecture and happenings as we are guided by musician Aniqa Qadir to create a soundscape. Working in groups, visitors will be prompted to absorb the sounds of the building and led through the process of creating an audio journey. This playful session welcomes participants to break the whisper norm of a library and interrupt the space with their observations. Led by Aniqa Qadir and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Thursday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. This year we are reading:  February 19 -  The Strangers by Katherena Vermette April 16 -  Big Little Lies by Liane Moriarty June 18 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 17 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 19 -  Small Things Like These by

events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around the Branch Tour
events.description: The Brookbanks branch and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry team to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood tour. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Free program.  Registration will open up one month before the program date. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.
